job_snippets: Build and deploy machine learning models for classification, regression, and clustering using scikit-learn, XGBoost, or LightGBM.
Develop deep learning architectures with PyTorch or TensorFlow for NLP, vision, or tabular tasks.
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) using Pandas, NumPy, and visualization libraries like Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly.
Design A/B experiments and statistical tests to measure feature impact; communicate results to stakeholders.
Build and maintain ML pipelines using MLflow, Kubeflow, or Airflow for model training and serving.
Write efficient SQL queries against data warehouses like Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift.
Clean, wrangle, and transform large datasets; handle missing values, outliers, and feature engineering.
Implement NLP pipelines: text preprocessing, tokenization, embeddings, and fine-tuning transformers (BERT, GPT).
Deploy ML models as REST APIs using FastAPI or Flask; integrate with production systems.
Collaborate with data engineers to ingest data from multiple sources into the feature store.
Use cloud ML platforms: AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, or Vertex AI for scalable training and inference.
Monitor model performance and data drift in production; implement retraining pipelines.
Create compelling data visualizations and dashboards using Tableau, Power BI, or Streamlit.
Apply causal inference methods: diff-in-diff, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity.
Strong background in probability, statistics, linear algebra, and calculus.
Version control for data and models: DVC, Git, experiment tracking with MLflow or Weights & Biases.
Work with unstructured data: images, text, audio; apply computer vision or speech recognition models.
Communicate findings clearly in Jupyter Notebooks, written reports, and presentations.
Experience with recommender systems, ranking models, or reinforcement learning is a plus.
Understand data governance, PII handling, and model fairness and bias considerations.
